title: Silky Roast Dutch Pork Roast
description: A comforting, silky pork roast dish, perfect for a sick day, featuring a sweet and sticky light rum glaze, and designed to be anti-inflammatory friendly.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with salt, pepper, and chopped thyme.
In a small bowl, mix together the honey, Dijon mustard, and cornstarch to make the glaze.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat, then brown the pork on all sides until golden brown, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside on a plate.
Reduce heat to medium and add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, water, and rum to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the browned pork to the pot and spoon some of the glaze over the top of the pork.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 2-1/2 to 3 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 190F (88C).
